title: MBTA announces schedule changes, detours as nearly 1 million expected to attend Sunday’s St. Patrick’s Day parade
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: MBTA will run Red Line on rush hour schedule from 10 a.m. to 6 p.m. on parade day.
Shuttle buses from South Station to South Boston will operate from 9:30 a.m. to 4:30 p.m.
Bus routes 9 and 11 suspended, and route detours will affect routes 10, 16, 17, and 47.

title: Boston Public Schools graduation rate reached all-time high last year, still lags behind state average
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Boston Public Schools' graduation rate reached a record high of 81.3% in 2025, a 1.6% increase from 2024.
The city graduation rate improved but remains eight points behind the Massachusetts average of 89.3%.
Notable improvements were observed among English learners and students with disabilities, each seeing over a 5% increase.

title: Bruins’ playoff hopes are destined to fail if they can’t solve confounding road woes
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Bruins hold a 11-14-5 road record this season, concerning given their playoff aspirations.
Boston's recent 5-4 overtime loss to Pittsburgh highlighted ongoing road struggles despite a three-goal lead.
With 11 crucial road games left, the Bruins are barely in a playoff spot with 76 points, closely pursued by the Blue Jackets.

title: This Marblehead man is running the Boston Marathon for his pediatric patients
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Brian Wishart, 42, from Marblehead, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support Spaulding Rehabilitation Hospital.
Wishart is the Chief of Pediatric Rehabilitation Medicine, focusing on integrating research and improving clinical care.
Funds raised will enhance resources for pediatric patients, including adaptive sports programs and child life specialist support.

title: 8 children have now died from flu in Mass. this season
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Eight children and 298 adults have died from influenza in Massachusetts this season, according to state data through Feb. 28.
Massachusetts public health officials report 38.1% of residents have been vaccinated against the flu as of late February.
Experts urge flu vaccination for individuals over six months old, despite the flu season's approaching end.

title: NBA cancels Hawks’ ‘Magic City’ promotion amid backlash following Luke Kornet’s letter
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: NBA cancels Atlanta Hawks' 'Magic City Monday' promotion after backlash from stakeholders and a viral letter by Luke Kornet.
The promotion featured Magic City strip club's chicken wings and merchandise, with hip-hop artist T.I. scheduled to perform.
Luke Kornet's letter raised concerns about family-friendly atmosphere, gaining support from some NBA players including Al Horford.

title: Shaquille O’Neal had no clue who Celtics guard Baylor Scheierman is
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Shaquille O'Neal on ESPN's Inside the NBA expressed unfamiliarity with Celtics guard Baylor Scheierman.
Baylor Scheierman, drafted in 2024, has appeared in 59 games this season, starting 16 times for the Celtics.
Scheierman averages 4.8 points, 3.2 rebounds, and is shooting 38.9% from three-point range this season.

title: Healey calls on aviation company operating at Hanscom to cut ties with ICE
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Governor Maura Healey urges Signature Aviation to end involvement with ICE deportation flights at Hanscom Field.
Signature Aviation is key to ICE operations at Hanscom, facilitating fuel, parking, and vehicle access for flights.
Healey cites ICE's rights violations and mass deportation efforts, requesting Signature to discontinue support at Hanscom.

title: Delivery person falls through a shaft outside Boston’s Omni Parker House
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: A delivery worker fell 20 feet through a shaft outside Boston's Omni Parker House after a metal grate collapsed.
Boston fire and police departments responded, with the injured worker conscious, later taken to hospital for evaluation.
Boston Inspection Services cited the hotel for a building code violation after finding rotted brackets caused the grate collapse.

title: Quincy gas station looking for violist to ‘serenade’ customers
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Quincy Gas owner Harvey Kertzman seeks violist to serenade customers during their visits.
Retired violist to start performing when spring weather warms, with signs still up for backups.
The gas station also provides complimentary bottled water and inspirational quotes on pumps.
